title: 1.1: Tests that SFTP client can successfully authenticate using public key when external auth scope allows both methods
1.2: Tests that SFTP client can successfully authenticate using password when external auth scope allows both methods
1.3: Tests that SFTP operations work after successful authentication with dual-method scope
1.4: Tests that user is created in data provider after successful external authentication with dual-method scope
1.5: Tests that invalid username is rejected when using external authentication with dual-method scope
1.6: Tests that password authentication succeeds when external auth scope is password-only
1.7: Tests that public key authentication is rejected when external auth scope is password-only
1.8: Tests that invalid credentials are rejected when external auth scope is password-only
1.9: Tests that public key authentication succeeds when external auth scope is public-key-only
1.10: Tests that password authentication is rejected when external auth scope is public-key-only
1.11: Tests that invalid credentials are rejected when external auth scope is public-key-only
2.1: Places the test for dual-method external authentication in the sftpd test directory
2.2: Places the test for password-only external authentication in the sftpd test directory
2.3: Places the test for public-key-only external authentication in the sftpd test directory
3.1: All tests use Go testing framework with *testing.T parameter
3.2: All tests follow the naming pattern starting with Test prefix
3.3: All tests use t.Errorf or t.Error for reporting test failures
3.4: All tests reinitialize the data provider after reconfiguring external auth settings
3.5: All tests restore the data provider configuration to original state after test completion
4.1: Creates an external auth script file for test setup
4.2: Cleans up external auth script file after test completion
